name: when-gathering-requirements-use-interactive-planner
description: Use Claude Code's AskUserQuestion tool to gather comprehensive requirements through structured multi-select questions.
capabilities: Guide users through 5-phase requirements gathering workflow
Use AskUserQuestion tool for structured multi-select questions
Organize responses into formal specifications document
Validate completeness with stakeholder approval steps
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only Markdown, YAML, JSON, and Graphviz files. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or command execution. All 18 static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown formatting, version strings were misidentified as crypto algorithms, and URLs are metadata references.
valueStatement: Gathering project requirements can be time-consuming and incomplete when done through linear conversation. This skill uses Claude Code's interactive question tool to systematically collect all necessary information through structured multi-select questions, ensuring nothing is missed.
limitations: Requires active user participation and responses
Does not execute code or access external systems
Relies on Claude Code's AskUserQuestion tool availability

useCases.title: Kick off new projects
useCases.description: Systematically gather all stakeholder requirements before project kickoff

useCases.title: Clarify feature requests
useCases.description: Extract detailed technical requirements from vague feature descriptions

useCases.title: Document specifications
useCases.description: Create comprehensive product specifications with clear acceptance criteria

promptTemplates.title: Basic Requirements
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to gather requirements for a new project. Please use the interactive planner to ask me initial discovery questions about project goals and scope.

promptTemplates.title: Technical Details
promptTemplates.prompt: Now follow up with detailed technical questions about scale, performance requirements, and technology constraints.

promptTemplates.title: Timeline and Resources
promptTemplates.prompt: Ask me about timeline expectations, resource constraints, and feature priorities using multi-select options.

promptTemplates.title: Final Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review all gathered requirements with me and create a final specifications document with action plan.

bestPractices: Start with open questions about project goals before diving into technical details
Use multi-select options for priorities to surface trade-offs early
Validate requirements with stakeholders before creating the action plan
antiPatterns: Skipping the discovery phase and jumping directly to technical questions
Using single-select when multiple priorities should be considered
Creating action plans without stakeholder validation

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Requires AskUserQuestion tool availability.

faq.question: How long does the full requirements process take?
faq.answer: Plan for 15-30 minutes. Shorter sessions may result in incomplete requirements.

faq.question: Can I resume a partially completed session?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the documented memory keys to retrieve previous responses and continue from where you left off.

faq.question: Is any user data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: No. Requirements are only stored in Claude Code's ephemeral context during the session.

faq.question: What if I get stuck or responses are unclear?
faq.answer: The workflow includes validation phases to catch gaps. Use follow-up questions to clarify ambiguous responses.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual requirements gathering?
faq.answer: Structured questions ensure nothing is missed. Multi-select options make priorities explicit. Stakeholder validation prevents misunderstandings.
